verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, filii, disciplinam patris,  et attendite ut sciatis prudentiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֣וּ בָ֭נִים מ֣וּסַר אָ֑ב וְ֝הַקְשִׁ֗יבוּ לָדַ֥עַת בִּינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend, that you may know prudence.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuulkaat, lapseni, isänne kuritusta, ja ottakaa vaari, oppiaksenne ja viisaammaksi tullaksenne.
verses.king_james: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Donum bonum tribuam vobis :  legem meam ne derelinquatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֶ֣קַח ט֭וֹב נָתַ֣תִּי לָכֶ֑ם תּ֝וֹרָתִ֗י אַֽל־תַּעֲזֹֽבוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: I will give you a good gift, forsake not my law.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minä annan teille hyvän opetuksen; älkööt hyljätkö minun lakiani.
verses.king_james: For I give you good doctrine, forsake ye not my law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et ego filius fui patris mei,  tenellus et unigenitus coram matre mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־בֵ֭ן הָיִ֣יתִי לְאָבִ֑י רַ֥ךְ וְ֝יָחִ֗יד לִפְנֵ֥י אִמִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I also was my father's son, tender, and as an only son in the sight of my mother:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minä olin isäni poika, hoikka ja ainoa äidilläni.
verses.king_james: For I was my father’s son, tender and only beloved in the sight of my mother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et docebat me, atque dicebat :  Suscipiat verba mea cor tuum ;  custodi præcepta mea, et vives.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּרֵ֗נִי וַיֹּ֥אמֶר לִ֗י יִֽתְמׇךְ־דְּבָרַ֥י לִבֶּ֑ךָ שְׁמֹ֖ר מִצְוֺתַ֣י וֶחְיֵֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he taught me, and said: Let thy heart receive my words, keep my commandments, and thou shalt live.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän opetti minua, ja sanoi minulle; anna sydämes ottaa minun sanani vastaan: pidä minun käskyni, niin sinä elää saat.
verses.king_james: He taught me also, and said unto me, Let thine heart retain my words: keep my commandments, and live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posside sapientiam, posside prudentiam :  ne obliviscaris, neque declines a verbis oris mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קְנֵ֣ה חׇ֭כְמָה קְנֵ֣ה בִינָ֑ה אַל־תִּשְׁכַּ֥ח וְאַל־תֵּ֝֗ט מֵאִמְרֵי־פִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Get wisdom, get prudence: forget not, neither decline from the words of my mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Osta viisaus, osat ymmärrys: älä unohda, älä myös poikkee minun puheestani.
verses.king_james: Get wisdom, get understanding: forget it not; neither decline from the words of my mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dimittas eam, et custodiet te :  dilige eam, et conservabit te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּעַזְבֶ֥הָ וְתִשְׁמְרֶ֑ךָּ אֱהָבֶ֥הָ וְתִצְּרֶֽךָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Forsake her not, and she shall keep thee: love her, and she shall preserve thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä häntä hylkää, niin hän sinut kätkee: rakasta häntä, niin hän sinua varjelee.
verses.king_james: Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principium sapientiæ : posside sapientiam,  et in omni possessione tua acquire prudentiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֵאשִׁ֣ית חׇ֭כְמָה קְנֵ֣ה חׇכְמָ֑ה וּבְכׇל־קִ֝נְיָנְךָ֗ קְנֵ֣ה בִינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The beginning of wisdom, get wisdom, and with all thy possession purchase prudence.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä viisauden alku on, ostaa viisautta, ja kaikella saadullas ostaa taitoa.
verses.king_james: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Arripe illam, et exaltabit te ;  glorificaberis ab ea cum eam fueris amplexatus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סַלְסְלֶ֥הָ וּֽתְרוֹמְמֶ֑ךָּ תְּ֝כַבֵּ֗דְךָ כִּ֣י תְחַבְּקֶֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Take hold on her, and she shall exalt thee: thou shalt be glorified by her, when thou shalt embrace her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pidä häntä korkiassa kunniassa, niin hän sinua korottaa, ja saattaa kunniaan, jos sinä häntä rakastat.
verses.king_james: Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dabit capiti tuo augmenta gratiarum,  et corona inclyta proteget te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּתֵּ֣ן לְ֭רֹאשְׁךָ לִוְיַת־חֵ֑ן עֲטֶ֖רֶת תִּפְאֶ֣רֶת תְּמַגְּנֶֽךָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: She shall give to thy head increase of graces, and protect thee with a noble crown.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sinun pääs jalosti kaunistaa, ja kunnioittaa sinua ihanalla kruunulla.
verses.king_james: She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audi, fili mi, et suscipe verba mea,  ut multiplicentur tibi anni vitæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ֭נִי וְקַ֣ח אֲמָרָ֑י וְיִרְבּ֥וּ לְ֝ךָ֗ שְׁנ֣וֹת חַיִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hear, O my son, and receive my words, that years of life may be multiplied to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuule siis, poikani, ja ota minun puheeni, niin ikäs vuotta on monta.
verses.king_james: Hear, O my son, and receive my sayings; and the years of thy life shall be many.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viam sapientiæ monstrabo tibi ;  ducam te per semitas æquitatis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ חׇ֭כְמָה הֹרֵיתִ֑יךָ הִ֝דְרַכְתִּ֗יךָ בְּמַעְגְּלֵי־יֹֽשֶׁר׃
verses.douay_rheims: I will shew thee the way of wisdom, I will lead thee by the paths of equity:
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä johdatan sinua viisauden tielle, ja saatan sinua käymään oikialla retkellä,
verses.king_james: I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in right paths.

verses.latin_vulgate: quas cum ingressus fueris, non arctabuntur gressus tui,  et currens non habebis offendiculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽ֭לֶכְתְּךָ לֹא־יֵצַ֣ר צַעֲדֶ֑ךָ וְאִם־תָּ֝ר֗וּץ לֹ֣א תִכָּשֵֽׁל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which when thou shalt have entered, thy steps shall not be straitened, and when thou runnest, thou shalt not meet a stumblingblock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin että koskas vaellat, ei sinun käymises ole ahdas; ja koskas juokset, niin et sinä loukkaa sinuas.
verses.king_james: When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tene disciplinam, ne dimittas eam ;  custodi illam, quia ipsa est vita tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַחֲזֵ֣ק בַּמּוּסָ֣ר אַל־תֶּ֑רֶף נִ֝צְּרֶ֗הָ כִּי־הִ֥יא חַיֶּֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Take hold on instruction, leave it not: keep it, because it is thy life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pidä kuritus, älä hylkää häntä: kätke häntä; sillä hän on sinun elämäs.
verses.king_james: Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne delecteris in semitis impiorum,  nec tibi placeat malorum via.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֹ֣רַח רְ֭שָׁעִים אַל־תָּבֹ֑א וְאַל־תְּ֝אַשֵּׁ֗ר בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ רָעִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Be not delighted in the paths of the wicked, neither let the way of evil men please thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä mene jumalattomain askelille, ja älä astu pahain tielle;
verses.king_james: Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuge ab ea, nec transeas per illam ;  declina, et desere eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּרָעֵ֥הוּ אַל־תַּעֲבׇר־בּ֑וֹ שְׂטֵ֖ה מֵעָלָ֣יו וַעֲבֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Flee from it, pass not by it: go aside, and forsake it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jätä se pois, ja älä käy siinä; karta sitä, ja mene ohitse.
verses.king_james: Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim dormiunt nisi malefecerint,  et rapitur somnus ab eis nisi supplantaverint.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א יִֽ֭שְׁנוּ אִם־לֹ֣א יָרֵ֑עוּ וְֽנִגְזְלָ֥ה שְׁ֝נָתָ֗ם אִם־לֹ֥א (יכשולו) [יַכְשִֽׁילוּ]׃
verses.douay_rheims: For they sleep not, except they have done evil: and their sleep is taken away unless they have made some to fall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä ei he makaa, jollei he ole pahoin tehneet, eikä lepää, jollei he ole vahinkoa tehneet.
verses.king_james: For they sleep not, except they have done mischief; and their sleep is taken away, unless they cause some to fall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Comedunt panem impietatis,  et vinum iniquitatis bibunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י לָ֭חֲמוּ לֶ֣חֶם רֶ֑שַׁע וְיֵ֖ין חֲמָסִ֣ים יִשְׁתּֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä he syövät jumalattomuuden leipää, ja juovat vääryyden viinaa.
verses.king_james: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Justorum autem semita quasi lux splendens procedit,  et crescit usque ad perfectam diem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֹ֣רַח צַ֭דִּיקִים כְּא֣וֹר נֹ֑גַהּ הוֹלֵ֥ךְ וָ֝א֗וֹר עַד־נְכ֥וֹן הַיּֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the path of the just, as a shining light, goeth forwards, and increaseth even to perfect day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta vanhurskasten retki paistaa niinkuin valkeus, käy edes, ja valistaa hamaan isoon päivään asti.
verses.king_james: But the path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Via impiorum tenebrosa ;  nesciunt ubi corruant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דֶּ֣רֶךְ רְ֭שָׁעִים כָּאֲפֵלָ֑ה לֹ֥א יָ֝דְע֗וּ בַּמֶּ֥ה יִכָּשֵֽׁלוּ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The way of the wicked is darksome: they know not where they fall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jumalattomain tie on niinkuin pimeys; ja ei he tiedä, kussa he lankeevat.
verses.king_james: The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fili mi, ausculta sermones meos,  et ad eloquia mea inclina aurem tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭נִי לִדְבָרַ֣י הַקְשִׁ֑יבָה לַ֝אֲמָרַ֗י הַט־אׇזְנֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: My son, hearken to my words, and incline thy ear to my sayings.
verses.finnish_biblia: Poikani, ota vaari sanoistani, ja kallista korvas puheisiini.
verses.king_james: My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne recedant ab oculis tuis :  custodi ea in medio cordis tui :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־יַלִּ֥יזוּ מֵעֵינֶ֑יךָ שׇׁ֝מְרֵ֗ם בְּת֣וֹךְ לְבָבֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let them not depart from thy eyes, keep them in the midst of thy heart:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä niiden anna tulla pois silmistäs: pidä ne sydämessäs.
verses.king_james: Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: vita enim sunt invenientibus ea,  et universæ carni sanitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־חַיִּ֣ים הֵ֭ם לְמֹצְאֵיהֶ֑ם וּֽלְכׇל־בְּשָׂר֥וֹ מַרְפֵּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: For they are life to those that find them, and health to all flesh.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä ne ovat niiden elämä, jotka niitä ovat löytäneet, ja ovat terveelliset koko heidän ruumiillensa.
verses.king_james: For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omni custodia serva cor tuum,  quia ex ipso vita procedit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽכׇּל־מִ֭שְׁמָר נְצֹ֣ר לִבֶּ֑ךָ כִּֽי־מִ֝מֶּ֗נּוּ תּוֹצְא֥וֹת חַיִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: With all watchfulness keep thy heart, because life issueth out from it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Varjele sydämes kaikella ahkeruudella: sillä siitä elämä tulee.
verses.king_james: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Remove a te os pravum,  et detrahentia labia sint procul a te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָסֵ֣ר מִ֭מְּךָ עִקְּשׁ֣וּת פֶּ֑ה וּלְז֥וּת שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם הַרְחֵ֥ק מִמֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Remove from thee a froward mouth, and let detracting lips be far from thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pane pois paha suu, ja väärät huulet anna olla sinustas kaukana.
verses.king_james: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculi tui recta videant,  et palpebræ tuæ præcedant gressus tuos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֭ינֶיךָ לְנֹ֣כַח יַבִּ֑יטוּ וְ֝עַפְעַפֶּ֗יךָ יַיְשִׁ֥רוּ נֶגְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let thy eyes look straight on, and let thy eyelids go before thy steps.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katsokoon silmäs oikein eteensä, ja sinun silmäs laudat olkoot oikiat edessäs.
verses.king_james: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dirige semitam pedibus tuis,  et omnes viæ tuæ stabilientur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פַּ֭לֵּס מַעְגַּ֣ל רַגְלֶ֑ךָ וְֽכׇל־דְּרָכֶ֥יךָ יִכֹּֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Make straight the path for thy feet, and all thy ways shall be established.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koettele jalkais askeleet, niin kaikki sinun ties vahvistuvat.
verses.king_james: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne declines ad dexteram neque ad sinistram ;  averte pedem tuum a malo :  vias enim quæ a dextris sunt novit Dominus :  perversæ vero sunt quæ a sinistris sunt.  Ipse autem rectos faciet cursus tuos,  itinera autem tua in pace producet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַֽל־תֵּט־יָמִ֥ין וּשְׂמֹ֑אול הָסֵ֖ר רַגְלְךָ֣ מֵרָֽע׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Decline not to the right hand, nor to the left: turn away thy foot from evil. For the Lord knoweth the ways that are on the right hand: but those are perverse which are on the left hand. But he will make thy courses straight, he will bring forward thy ways in peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä poikkee oikialle eli vasemmalle puolelle: tempaa jalkas pois pahuudesta.
verses.king_james: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil.
